title: Public-site presentation polish and about page (no data surface changes)
problem: The public site is structurally real but still reads as a test artifact. It exposes raw enum strings and raw ISO timestamps, lacks an /about methodology and trust page, has default 404 behavior, no explicit empty state, minimal metadata/favicons, and inline styling debt. This should be presentation-only and must not broaden the public data surface.
acceptance_criteria: Raw enum values are humanized in public UI without changing the export schema.
Public timestamps are formatted for humans while preserving safe public values.
/about exists and explains the engine, fixture provenance, confidence semantics, read-only safety boundary, and public/private data split.
A custom 404 page and zero-card empty state use the public site's visual language.
No new public export fields are added.
No raw source text, prompts, secrets, local paths, private notes, hidden reasoning, injected instructions, unreviewed draft internals, or internal IDs are rendered.
Public site remains static and read-only.
Existing GT12 and GT25 still pass, with tests updated only for presentation/page coverage.
Full golden tests and safety audit pass.
non_goals: Do not change the public export schema.
Do not add new public data fields.
Do not change runtime pipeline behavior.
Do not add live provider or Ollama work.
Do not add public write routes.
Do not implement ticket-037 or later.
